pr_title: [DOM-55102] - Create scaffolding for bootstrapping model-serving microservice

comments.author: fernandoacorreia
comments.body:
These settings come from nucleus and their default values are defined there. They must not be defined on this file.

Here we will only add settings that are specific to the model-serving service and are not being inherited from nucleus.

Those settings will start with the `modelServing.` prefix instead of `com.cerebro.domino.`

comments.author: fernandoacorreia
comments.body: @ddl-tnguyen unless I'm missing something I still think that settings that are retrieved from nucleus shouldn't be defined on the model-serving configuration file.

comments.author: ddl-tnguyen
comments.body:
If those settings are not explicitly set by an admin in central config, they actually do not end up in the `Configuration` object we get from nucleus and will throw an error if you try to `.get` those values. 

In [these lines in Settings.scala](https://github.com/cerebrotech/domino/blob/76ce145a07888e82633931e4ab3eca3c3de3c081/server/app/domino/server/lib/config/Settings.scala#L569C7-L574C8) nucleus checks `Configuration` for those optional values and otherwise sets them to the default.

My logic was that we should store those default values in the model-serving `application.conf` file, so that we can try to get the central config from the nucleus `Configuration` and otherwise fall back on the defaults defined in the model-serving `Configuration`. If an admin sets any of those values in central config, it will override the default. This is what `ModelServingConfigLoader.scala` ends up doing.

pr_title: [DOM-54217] move develop scheduled / periodic tasks into nucleus-develop deployment

comments.author: ddl-ryan-connor
comments.body: where is this used?

comments.author: ddl-mhito
comments.body:
This is used in the workspace volume cleaner, which takes in injected workspace settings from here:

```
    WorkspaceSettings(
      stateTransitionRequestValidityDuration = settings.Workbench.Workspace.stateTransitionRequestValidityDuration,
      dependencyRetrievalCacheTimeout = settings.Workbench.Workspace.dependencyRetrievalCacheTimeout,
      quotaSettings = quotaSettings,
      notifierSettings = WorkspaceNotifierSettings(
        isDispatcher = settings.Dispatcher.isEnabled,
        messagePrefetchCount = settings.Workbench.Workspace.Notifier.messagePrefetchCount,
        maxMessageCount = settings.Workbench.Workspace.Notifier.maxMessageCount
      ),
      stopToDeleteDelayDuration = settings.Workbench.Workspace.stopToDeleteDelayDuration,
      workspaceLogFetchLimit = settings.logRetriever.runLogFetchSize,
      userHost = settings.userHost,
      idlePeriodThresholdDays = settings.Workbench.Workspace.idlePeriodThresholdDays,
      idlePeriodThresholdMinutes = settings.Workbench.Workspace.idlePeriodThresholdMinutes,
      highDiskUsageThresholdPercent = settings.Workbench.Workspace.Volume.highDiskUsageThresholdPercent,
      enabledPVCleanup = settings.Workbench.Workspace.enabledPVCleanup,
      unusedGracePeriodDays = settings.Workbench.Workspace.unusedGracePeriodDays,
      unusedGracePeriodMinutes = settings.Workbench.Workspace.unusedGracePeriodMinutes,
      cleanupFrequencyDays = settings.Workbench.Workspace.cleanupFrequencyDays,
      globalSettingsAnonymousAccessEnabled = settings.endpointsWithAnonymousUsersEnabled.workspaceGlobalSettings,
      isInScope = settings.nucleusScopes match {
          case Some(scopes) => scopes.contains(NucleusScopes.Develop)
          case None => settings.Spof.isEnabled
        },
    )
```

I made this separate from `enaledPVCleanup` to make it clear it has to do with deployment tags rather than central config values.
